key_departures.impact: Released at start of new league year. Cousins had led the Falcons to a 4-game winning streak to close 2025 after Penix's ACL tear, finishing 8-9. His $22.5M dead cap hit eats into ATL's 2026 space. With Penix recovering from torn ACL and timeline uncertain, QB was immediately ATL's top need.

Team captain and tackling leader (107 combined tackles, 3.5 sacks, 10 TFLs, 1 INT in 2025). Led ATL in tackles for multiple seasons. Three-year, $33M deal with Saints ($23M guaranteed) nearly doubles his previous ATL salary. A genuine starter-level loss at ILB — described as losing 'more than one human being' worth of production.

Started 47 of 48 games in three ATL seasons. 157 combined tackles, 8 sacks, 21 TFLs over his tenure. Primary run-stopper on a defensive line that ranked 27th in EPA per rush allowed in 2025. Departure directly escalates interior DL need.

Second-round pick who never fully lived up to billing but was a rotational contributor. Departure adds to an already fragile EDGE group with James Pearce Jr. facing felony charges and uncertain availability.
Solid depth player with 50 tackles, 11 TFLs, 4 sacks in 25 games (5 starts) with ATL over two seasons. Departure thins interior DL rotation further following Onyemata loss.

Had an outstanding 2025 stepping in for injured Billy Bowman Jr. Inside-outside versatility. Signed a reported 3-year deal worth up to $21M with $10M guaranteed in Buffalo. Departure creates a real CB2/nickel hole.
Cut by Falcons before free agency; was carrying $18.4M cap hit. Injury-marred 2025 (32 catches, 443 yards). Signed 1-year deal with Giants. Departure creates/confirms WR2 need.
Cut with Mooney. Depth-level loss only.
Signed 2-year, $12.25M deal with Cardinals. Was Bijan Robinson's backup. Bijan Robinson remains, so RB is not a significant need.
Interior OL depth piece following OL coach Dwayne Ledford to Baltimore. Depth-only loss — starter IOL (Lindstrom, Neuzil) unchanged.
Veteran rotational edge rusher who mentored the young EDGE group in 2025. Another departure from an already thin pass-rush rotation.

signings.analysis: Veteran minimum deal possible only because Miami owes Tagovailoa $54M in 2026 guaranteed salary; Falcons pay just the minimum with offset language. A legitimate QB1-caliber player on paper who provides a starter-level option while Penix recovers from torn ACL. However, this is a 1-year deal with rock-bottom guarantees — a bridge and competition arrangement, not a long-term solution. Tagovailoa's 2025 was poor (career-high 15 INTs, benched), so there are real performance concerns. QB need is partially addressed for 2026 but the long-term question remains entirely open pending Penix's health and development.

Franchise tagged at $15.045M — keeps ATL's breakout TE for 2026 after a strong 2025 season. Not a need-addressing move (TE was not a need), but a significant roster retention. A long-term extension is expected before or during the 2026 season. The tag itself adds $15M in cap pressure.

Two-year deal worth up to $17M ($10M guaranteed) for the former 16th overall pick. At $7.5M AAV this is a mid-tier-to-depth signing at WR — well below the $15M+ starter threshold. Dotson is 25 and had legitimate production (523 yards, 7 TDs in his rookie year) but has never replicated it, posting just 37 catches for 478 yards in 2 seasons as a bit player in Philly. He's expected to compete for the WR2 slot behind Drake London. Two years of guarantees is a real investment, and the Falcons are clearly betting on a bounce-back. But at this price point, he's a WR2 candidate, not a proven WR1. Combined with Zaccheaus (depth), WR need is partially addressed — ATL still needs more receiver talent via the draft.

Reunion signing for a familiar face who knows the org well under new GM Cunningham (Chicago) and president Matt Ryan (former ATL QB). One-year deal — pure depth. Zaccheaus provides slot versatility and special teams value (17 punt returns in 2024). Projects as WR3 or WR4 at best. Does nothing to address the WR2 starter need. Contract terms not fully disclosed but consistent with veteran minimum/low-end depth deal.

Reunion signing — Hooper was ATL's starting TE from 2016-2019 and had career highs (75 catches, 787 yards) in his final ATL season under Matt Ryan. One-year, $3.25M deal. New HC Stefanski loves 12-personnel, so pairing Hooper with Pitts in 2-TE sets makes sense schematically. TE was not a need entering FA with Pitts retained. Hooper adds quality veteran depth at an affordable price.

Georgia native (high school in Marietta) returning home on a 1-year deal. Ojulari had 22 career sacks in 5 seasons with Giants and Eagles, but 2025 was cut short by injury. Projects into the edge rotation alongside Jalon Walker. One-year deal signals a prove-it arrangement. This is NOT a starter-level signing at EDGE — it's rotational depth. Combined with the Pearce situation (felony charges) and Ebiketie/Floyd departures, EDGE remains a significant draft need. Contract value estimated from position market context; exact terms not fully disclosed.

Veteran rotational edge (35 career sacks) coming off injury-shortened 2025. His last fully healthy season (2023) produced 9.5 sacks for Indianapolis. At 31 and post-Achilles, there's uncertainty around his production level. One-year, undisclosed deal — consistent with low-end depth signing. Adds rotational insurance to a group already fragile because of James Pearce Jr.'s criminal charges. ATL has now accumulated Ojulari + Ebukam + Cameron Thomas + Elijah Garcia (tendered) around Walker at EDGE/DE, but none are proven starters. EDGE remains a clear draft priority.

Former Cardinals 3rd-round pick (2022), spent last 2 seasons in Cleveland under Stefanski. Had 2.5 sacks in 2025 as rotational player. Familiarity with the coaching staff is the key selling point. One-year, $3.1M deal. Solid depth/rotation piece but not a starter. Part of the collective rotation-building effort at EDGE — individually minimal impact on the need.

One-year, $3M deal. Hand is 6'3, 300+ lbs — exactly the run-stuffer profile ATL desperately needed after ranking 27th in EPA per rush allowed in 2025. He had 5 TFLs in each of the past two seasons and started 13 games for the Chargers in 2025. Addresses Onyemata's departure directly in terms of role. But at $3M on a 1-year deal, this is a rotational/depth-tier commitment, not a starter-level IDL investment. IDL need is partially addressed (run-stuffing), but the depth overall remains thin. Draft capital at IDL still warranted.

Re-signed — first Falcons player from the 2025 roster retained by the new staff, indicating his value. Had 5 sacks and 7 TFLs in 13 games (5 starts) in 2025 as the team's most potent nose tackle. Deal terms modest (estimated from position context). Re-signing London alongside Hand partially addresses the IDL run-stuffing need lost with Onyemata, but both are depth/rotational — not top-of-market investments. IDL still needs fortification via the draft.

Undrafted free agent who spent 2 years with the Bears under new GM Cunningham. 4 sacks and 37 tackles over the last 2 seasons in Chicago. One-year deal — pure depth to fill out the IDL rotation. GM familiarity is the primary driver. Minimal impact on need.

One-year deal worth up to $3M. Former Texans LB with athleticism and coverage traits after 4 seasons in Houston. Has shown real promise in coverage but hasn't lived up to full potential. With Elliss gone and Divine Deablo as the only reliable ILB option, Harris gets a shot to compete for a starting role but this is a prove-it deal, not a starter-level commitment. LB remains a major need — Elliss's departure (107 tackles, defensive linchpin) is far more than Harris can replace on a 1-year, $3M arrangement. LB is still a top draft priority.

Former Georgia Bulldog and 2022 Dolphins 3rd-round pick. Primarily a special teams contributor with some defensive upside due to elite speed. One-year reported deal. Provides LB depth and ST help for an Atlanta ST unit that was one of the worst in the NFL in 2025. Does not address the starter-level void left by Elliss. Minimal impact on LB need.

Two-year, $9M deal (~$4.5M guaranteed). ATL used 3 kickers in 2025 (Koo, Romo, Gonzalez) — one of the worst ST units in the NFL. Folk is the NFL's most accurate kicker over the past 3 seasons (85% career FG%, 96.9% PAT) and worked with new ST coordinator Craig Aukerman in Tennessee in 2024 (95.5% FG%). The 2-year structure at this price signals a real investment in ST stability. K need fully addressed.

Three-year, $9M deal ($5M guaranteed) — a real multi-year commitment at punter. Bailey is a former All-Pro (2020, 48.7 yards/punt) who has punted well for Patriots and Dolphins. Atlanta is replacing Bradley Pinion (who was actually a bright spot) with an upgrade in Bailey. The 3-year, $5M guaranteed structure signals genuine investment. Punter need fully addressed.

One-year deal, reunites with OL coach Bill Callahan who coached him in Tennessee. Veteran backup C/G (88 career games, 7 starts) who can play center and both guard spots. Directly replaces Jovaughn Gwyn (who left for Baltimore to follow OL coach Ledford). IOL starter group (Lindstrom, Neuzil, etc.) is unchanged — this is pure depth. IOL not a critical need; depth slightly fortified.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Tagovailoa signing (1yr/$1.215M minimum) provides a legitimate QB1 candidate — experienced starter with a real shot to open 2026 as the starter while Penix recovers from torn ACL. However, this is a 1-year minimum deal — a bridge, not a solution. Penix's return timeline is unclear, Tagovailoa's 2025 was poor, and neither player has locked down the role long-term. QB drops from critical Tier 1 to Tier 2 — ATL could still target a QB early in the draft or via trade if available, but the urgency is reduced for 2026.

Dotson (2yr/$15M, $10M gtd) + Zaccheaus (1yr, depth) partially rebuild the WR room behind Drake London. Dotson is a real WR2 candidate with upside at just 25, and the $10M guarantee is a meaningful commitment. But Dotson's track record is underwhelming and no signing truly qualifies as a proven WR2. WR drops from Tier 1 to Tier 2 — still a meaningful draft target (rounds 1-3).

Elliss's departure (3yr/$33M with Saints) is a massive loss — he led the team in tackles multiple years and was irreplaceable as a two-down/three-down defender. The response of Christian Harris (1yr/$3M) and Channing Tindall (minimum) is wholly inadequate relative to the production lost. LB escalates from Tier 2 to Tier 1 — must address in the first three rounds of the draft alongside veteran FA additions.

ATL has added Ojulari (1yr, rotational), Ebukam (1yr, returning from Achilles), and Cameron Thomas (1yr, $3.1M) around Jalon Walker (2025 rookie). Collectively, this rotation-building effort raises the floor meaningfully. However, all three signings are 1-year, depth-tier deals. James Pearce Jr. (felony charges) is likely unavailable. Ebiketie and Floyd are gone. No true starter has been signed at EDGE. EDGE stays at Tier 2 — still a premium draft position in rounds 1-3.

ATL lost Onyemata (starter) and Street (rotational) but added Hand (1yr/$3M run-stopper), re-signed London (1yr, rotational), and added Williams (1yr, depth). The additions address the run-stuffing need at a functional level, but all are 1-year deals and none carry starter-tier investment. IDL remains Tier 2 — specifically still needs a young, cost-controlled IDL piece via the draft.

Dee Alford's departure to Buffalo (3yr/$21M, $10M gtd) is a significant loss of a versatile, ascending CB2/nickel who had an excellent 2025. ATL retained Natrone Brooks (extended) and Elijah Garcia (tendered), but neither is a reliable starter. CB has NOT been addressed in free agency. Escalates from Tier 2 to Tier 1 — need a starting-caliber CB in the draft (rounds 1-3).
Levin replaces Gwyn like-for-like as backup C/G depth. Starting IOL group unchanged (Lindstrom, Neuzil). No material change to IOL tier.
Matthews restructured (cleared $10.5M, effectively committed through 2027). McGary still on roster. OT starter group intact. Minor depth concern only.

Kyle Pitts franchise tagged ($15.045M) — ATL retains its ascending starting TE. Hooper (1yr/$3.25M) adds veteran TE2 depth. Stefanski's TE-heavy scheme is well-served. TE need essentially resolved — Tier 4 luxury pick only.
Jessie Bates III and Divine Deablo returning. No meaningful additions or subtractions at safety. Need unchanged.

key_additions.headline: Former Pro Bowl QB signs minimum deal to compete with Penix Jr. as Atlanta's bridge starter
Former 16th overall pick and Super Bowl champion gets fresh start as ATL's WR2 alongside Drake London
Breakout TE retained via franchise tag; long-term extension expected during 2026 season
NFL's most accurate kicker (last 3 seasons) ends ATL's carousel nightmare at placekicker
All-Pro punter (2020) provides long-term stability in total special teams overhaul
Former All-Pro TE reunites with Matt Ryan's Falcons for TE2 role in Stefanski's 12-personnel system
300+ lb run-stopper directly addresses ATL's worst 2025 weakness — 27th in EPA per rush allowed
Georgia native returns home on prove-it deal to join young EDGE rotation around Jalon Walker
Veteran pass rusher (35 career sacks, 9.5 in 2023) provides upside depth amid James Pearce Jr. uncertainty
Athletic LB with coverage upside gets starter opportunity after Kaden Elliss's departure to Saints
Fan-favorite UDFA returns for third stint with Falcons as WR3/slot depth behind London and Dotson

key_losses.headline: Veteran QB cut as new regime moves to Tua/Penix era; $22.5M dead cap hit lingers
ATL's defensive anchor (107 tackles, 3.5 sacks in 2025) nearly doubles his salary returning to Saints
Three-year starting DT departs, leaving critical run-stopping void on a defense ranked 27th vs. the run
Ascending versatile CB2/nickel cashes in after outstanding 2025, leaving ATL's secondary short-handed
Cut after injury-plagued 2025 (32 catches, 443 yards) to avoid $18.4M cap hit
Second-round pick heads to Philly as ATL and Eagles swap pass rushers (Ojulari for Ebiketie)
